name: Departures
description: Departures-style board showing upcoming events from an iCalendar feed with configurable Go/No-Go status indicators.
author: Adam

calendar_url.title: iCalendar URL
calendar_url.description: Public or private .ics feed URL. Supports https:// and webcal:// formats.

indicator_1.title: Go/No-Go Indicator 1 (Time)
indicator_1.description: Key to look for in calendar event notes (e.g. FLIGHT). Must contain a 24-hour time value (e.g. FLIGHT:1045 or FLIGHT:10:45) — this drives the hour-by-hour countdown when departure is less than 24 hours away. Green tile when a valid time is set; red when missing or invalid.

indicator_2.title: Go/No-Go Indicator 2
indicator_2.description: Key to look for in calendar event notes (e.g. STAY). Any value other than no/false/0/blank shows green. Leave empty to hide this indicator.

indicator_3.title: Go/No-Go Indicator 3
indicator_3.description: Key to look for in calendar event notes (e.g. PAID). Any value other than no/false/0/blank shows green. Leave empty to hide this indicator.

display_rows.title: Display Rows
display_rows.description: Number of events to show per page (1–6). The display cycles through all events this many at a time.

cycle_seconds.title: Cycle events (seconds)
cycle_seconds.description: How long each page of events is shown before advancing to the next.

lookback_days.title: Lookback Days
lookback_days.description: Number of days in the past to still display an event (0 = today only)

refresh_seconds.title: Calendar refresh (seconds)
refresh_seconds.description: How often to re-fetch the calendar feed.

row_1.description: Pre-composed 22-tile display row for the first event on the current page. Use row_2, row_3, etc. for additional rows per page.

current_page.description: Current page number in the cycle (1-based).

total_pages.description: Total number of pages based on event count and display_rows setting.

event_count.description: Total number of upcoming events tracked.

screenshots.caption: Departures-board layout showing upcoming events with Go/No-Go indicators
